trivia_bullets.text: Merchant of the Vale is a double-faced Adventure card. The adventure half, Haggle, is a one-mana instant that lets you optionally discard a card to draw a card. Adventure was new to ELD, letting players cast the instant/sorcery half first, exile it, then cast the creature from exile.
"Buying old, worn-out junk. Selling marvels of real value." — No named speaker; the line characterizes the fairytale-peddler archetype whose wares are worth more than they appear.

Haggle is a one-mana loot effect at instant speed — unusually efficient for red. Merchant of the Vale became a draft staple and saw fringe competitive play as a discard outlet that leaves a 2/3 body waiting in exile.

Creature type Human Peasant places the merchant outside all five courts — a commoner operating in the interstitial spaces of Eldraine. 'The Vale' is a common ELD locale signifying settled rural territory away from the courts.

related_cards.name: Lucky Clover
related_cards.note: Artifact that copies Adventure spells; Merchant's Haggle becomes two loot effects through it

related_cards.name: Bonecrusher Giant
related_cards.note: Adventure rare used as a loot/draw-comparison benchmark in ELD draft
